sanskrit:
राजविद्या राजगुह्यं पवित्रमिदमुत्तमम्।
प्रत्यक्षावगमं धर्म्यं सुसुखं कर्तुमव्ययम्॥
transliteration:
rāja-vidyā rāja-guhyaṃ pavitram idam uttamam |
pratyakṣāvagamaṃ dharmyaṃ su-sukhaṃ kartum avyayam ||
literal_meaning: This is the king of knowledge, the king of secrets, the purest, the highest; directly realizable, righteous, easy to perform, and imperishable.
contextual_meaning: Krishna presents a meta-cognitive upgrade: the highest knowledge is not information but direct, felt recognition of the imperishable Self, a practice so intrinsically rewarding that it becomes its own positive reinforcement loop.
psychological_state: A state of eustress—energized engagement where the seeker senses an imminent, joyful breakthrough that simultaneously dissolves old cognitive frames.
trigger_scenario: A high-performing professional in burnout suddenly discovers that the ‘next level’ is not another credential but an inner shift that makes daily life feel effortlessly meaningful.
anchor_text: Know the Self directly—pure, imperishable joy.
